relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Jesus crosses back into his own city.
Trust carries the paralyzed man, and Jesus speaks release before movement.
The scribes hear release as threat.
Jesus reads the heart beneath the objection.
Release and rising are joined.
The Son of Humanity shows authority on earth by restoring movement.
The man rises and returns home.
The crowd glorifies the Divine for authority given among humans.
Matthew is called from the tax table into following.
The table opens to tax collectors, sinners, disciples, and Jesus together.
Religious boundary-keepers question the table.
Jesus names himself physician for the sick.
Mercy is placed above sacrifice; the misaligned are called toward restoration.
John’s disciples ask about fasting.
The bridegroom image frames presence as celebration and absence as fasting.
New cloth cannot simply patch the old.
New wine needs fresh containers so both wine and skin are preserved.
A ruler asks Jesus to place his hand where death has entered.
Jesus rises and follows him.
A woman reaches through years of suffering to touch the fringe.
Her trust says even a garment-touch can carry healing.
Jesus sees her, calls her daughter, and names trust as healing.
Jesus enters the house of mourning.
He speaks of sleep where others see finality.
He takes her hand, and the girl rises.
The report spreads through the land.
Two blind men cry for mercy from the Son of David.
Jesus asks whether they trust his ability.
Their sight opens according to trust.
Their eyes open under his touch.
They cannot keep the story contained.
A muted man is brought in deep trouble.
When the troubling force leaves, speech returns and the crowd marvels.
The Pharisees interpret the release through suspicion.
Jesus moves through cities and villages teaching, proclaiming, and healing.
Compassion moves in his inward parts when he sees harassed sheep without a shepherd.
The harvest is abundant, but workers are few.
The disciples are told to ask for workers to be sent into the harvest.
